message:
fix(malloc/large): reject page_align overflow instead of wrapping

`page_align(size)` rounded up to the next page via
`(size + PAGE_SIZE - 1) & !(PAGE_SIZE - 1)`. For any `size` within
`PAGE_SIZE - 1` of `usize::MAX` the addition wraps to a very small
value, so the function silently returned a "rounded" size that was
*smaller* than the input. `LargeAllocator::alloc` then treated that
as a valid mapped size, updated `next_base` by the wrapped value,
and reported success — effectively an integer-overflow-driven unsafe
allocation footprint.

This change:

- Returns `Option<usize>` from `page_align` and uses
  `size.checked_add(PAGE_SIZE - 1)` before the mask, returning
  `None` on overflow.
- Threads the `Option` through `LargeAllocator::alloc` with `?`, so
  oversized requests now cleanly return `None` alongside the other
  `return None` guards already in the function.
- Adjusts the existing `test_page_alignment` cases to the new
  signature (`Some(4096)`, `Some(0)`, etc.) and adds
  `page_align(usize::MAX)` → `None`.
- Adds `test_large_alloc_rejects_page_align_overflow`: calls
  `allocator.alloc(usize::MAX)`, asserts it returns `None`, and
  confirms `active_count()` and `total_mapped()` remained at 0 so
  the failure path does not leak accounting state.

Only callers inside this crate touched page_align; this is not a
public API change. Fixes a latent overflow UB hazard reachable from
any huge `malloc`/`calloc` request that reaches the large-allocator
tier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
